---
title: "DM_R036: ETL Script Return Value Patterns"
date: "2025-08-28"
author: "Claude Code"
categories: ["Rules", "ETL", "Data Management", "Resource Cleanup"]
tags: ["etl", "return-values", "autodeinit", "pipeline-orchestration", "cleanup"]
---

## Rule Statement

**ETL scripts that need to return values for pipeline orchestration MUST use the five-part script structure (DEV_R032), which elegantly separates SUMMARIZE from DEINITIALIZE, enabling full variable access for reporting before cleanup.**

## Rationale

The `autodeinit()` function removes ALL user-created variables from the global environment. Any attempt to reference variables after `autodeinit()` will fail with "object not found" errors. The five-part structure solves this by separating reporting/return value preparation (SUMMARIZE) from cleanup (DEINITIALIZE).

## Implementation Pattern

### Mandatory Five-Part Structure

The five-part script structure (DEV_R032) is the required standard that completely eliminates autodeinit() problems:

```r
# ✅ BEST PRACTICE: Five-part structure with clean separation
# ... ETL processing in MAIN ...

# ==============================================================================
# PART 4: SUMMARIZE
# ==============================================================================
# All variables still accessible for reporting and return prep
final_metrics <- list(
  success = !main_error && test_passed,
  rows_processed = nrow(df_sales),
  execution_time = as.numeric(Sys.time() - start_time, units = "secs")
)

message(sprintf("📊 Processed %d rows", final_metrics$rows_processed))

# Prepare return value (variables still exist)
final_return_status <- final_metrics$success

# Optional: Save to disk for external monitoring
saveRDS(final_metrics, "etl_metrics.rds")

# ==============================================================================
# PART 5: DEINITIALIZE
# ==============================================================================
# Only cleanup, no variable access needed
autodeinit()  # Safe as the last statement
```

**Key Benefits:**
- ✅ **Architecturally Correct** - Complete separation of concerns
- ✅ **Full Variable Access** - All variables available in SUMMARIZE
- ✅ **Clean Code** - Maintainable and self-documenting
- ✅ **Universal Solution** - Works for all ETL patterns
- ✅ **No Workarounds Needed** - Natural solution, not a hack

## Anti-Patterns to Avoid

### Anti-Pattern 1: Variable Reference After autodeinit()

```r
# ❌ INCORRECT: Will always fail
final_status <- compute_status()
autodeinit()
return(final_status)  # ERROR: final_status doesn't exist
```

### Anti-Pattern 2: Capturing Before But Using After

```r
# ❌ INCORRECT: Misleading - appears correct but fails
# Capture before cleanup
return_value <- df_sales$status[1]

# Cleanup
autodeinit()

# Try to return
invisible(return_value)  # ERROR: return_value was removed
```

### Anti-Pattern 3: Conditional Return After autodeinit()

```r
# ❌ INCORRECT: Both branches will fail
autodeinit()

if (success_flag) {  # ERROR: success_flag doesn't exist
  return(TRUE)
} else {
  return(FALSE)
}
```

## Implementation Requirements

All ETL scripts MUST:

1. Use the five-part structure (DEV_R032)
2. Place all return value preparation in SUMMARIZE (Part 4)
3. Keep DEINITIALIZE (Part 5) for cleanup only
4. Ensure autodeinit() is the absolute last statement

## Integration with Other Principles

### DEV_R032 (Script Structure Standard Rule)
The five-part structure is the mandatory standard for all ETL scripts. It provides the only architecturally correct solution by separating SUMMARIZE from DEINITIALIZE.

### MP103 (autodeinit Behavior)
This rule implements the ETL-specific guidance from MP103, providing concrete patterns for handling the complete variable cleanup behavior.

### MP064 (ETL Derivation Separation)
ETL scripts in the 0IM/1ST/2TR pattern must choose appropriate cleanup based on their role in the pipeline.

### MP102 (ETL Output Standardization)
Standardized outputs must be preserved using the SUMMARIZE section when autodeinit() is needed.

## Validation

```r
# Function to check ETL script compliance
validate_etl_cleanup <- function(script_path) {
  lines <- readLines(script_path)
  
  # Check for five-part structure
  parts <- c("INITIALIZE", "MAIN", "TEST", "SUMMARIZE", "DEINITIALIZE")
  found_parts <- sapply(parts, function(p) {
    any(grepl(sprintf("PART [1-5]: %s", p), lines))
  })
  
  if (!all(found_parts)) {
    return(list(
      status = "ERROR",
      message = "Must use five-part structure",
      missing = parts[!found_parts]
    ))
  }
  
  # Find autodeinit calls
  autodeinit_lines <- grep("autodeinit\\(\\)", lines)
  
  if (length(autodeinit_lines) == 0) {
    return(list(status = "WARNING", message = "No autodeinit() found"))
  }
  
  # Check for return/invisible after last autodeinit
  last_autodeinit <- max(autodeinit_lines)
  remaining_lines <- lines[(last_autodeinit + 1):length(lines)]
  
  has_return <- any(grepl("^\\s*(return|invisible)\\s*\\(", remaining_lines))
  has_var_ref <- any(grepl("\\w+", remaining_lines[!grepl("^\\s*#|^\\s*$", remaining_lines)]))
  
  if (has_return || has_var_ref) {
    return(list(
      status = "ERROR",
      message = "Found return/variable reference after autodeinit()",
      line = last_autodeinit
    ))
  }
  
  return(list(status = "OK", message = "Correct cleanup pattern"))
}
```

## Example: Complete ETL Script

```r
#!/usr/bin/env Rscript
# cbz_ETL_sales_processing.R

# ==============================================================================
# PART 1: INITIALIZE
# ==============================================================================
source("scripts/global_scripts/22_initializations/sc_initialization_etl_mode.R")
con <- DBI::dbConnect(duckdb::duckdb(), "sales.duckdb")
main_error <- FALSE
test_passed <- FALSE

# ==============================================================================
# PART 2: MAIN
# ==============================================================================
tryCatch({
  df_raw <- DBI::dbReadTable(con, "raw_sales")
  df_processed <- process_sales_data(df_raw)
  DBI::dbWriteTable(con, "processed_sales", df_processed, overwrite = TRUE)
}, error = function(e) {
  message("Error in MAIN: ", e$message)
  main_error <- TRUE
})

# ==============================================================================
# PART 3: TEST
# ==============================================================================
if (!main_error) {
  test_passed <- DBI::dbExistsTable(con, "processed_sales")
}

# ==============================================================================
# PART 4: SUMMARIZE
# ==============================================================================
# Prepare return values and metrics
final_metrics <- list(
  success = test_passed && !main_error,
  rows_processed = if(exists("df_processed")) nrow(df_processed) else 0,
  timestamp = Sys.time()
)

# Save for pipeline orchestration
saveRDS(final_metrics, "etl_status.rds")
message(sprintf("ETL completed: %s", ifelse(final_metrics$success, "SUCCESS", "FAILED")))

# ==============================================================================
# PART 5: DEINITIALIZE
# ==============================================================================
DBI::dbDisconnect(con)
autodeinit()
```

## Compliance Checklist

- [ ] Using five-part structure (DEV_R032)
- [ ] SUMMARIZE (Part 4) contains all reporting and return prep
- [ ] DEINITIALIZE (Part 5) contains ONLY cleanup
- [ ] autodeinit() is the last statement in DEINITIALIZE
- [ ] No variable references after autodeinit()
- [ ] Return values prepared in SUMMARIZE before cleanup

## Changelog

### 2025-08-28: Simplified to Current Standard Only
- Removed all legacy workaround patterns per MP105
- Focused solely on five-part structure as mandatory standard
- Updated references from DEV_R033 to DEV_R032
- Cleaned up decision tree and compliance requirements

### 2025-08-28: Initial Creation
- Created rule to address systematic autodeinit() misuse in ETL scripts
- Documented five-part structure as the solution
- Added validation function for compliance checking

---

**Priority**: CRITICAL - Affects all ETL pipeline scripts
**Scope**: All ETL scripts MUST use five-part structure
**Validation**: Five-part structure required for deployment
